summary_one_line: Input must place the literal keyword 'ANY' right after '<!ELEMENT name ' so RAW/NXT(1)/NXT(2) equal 'A','N','Y'.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: After a well-formed '<!ELEMENT <name> ' element-declaration prefix, the next three content bytes must be the exact ASCII keyword 'A','N','Y' (0x41 0x4E 0x59) so the chained equality test RAW=='A' && NXT(1)=='N' && NXT(2)=='Y' at parser.c:6728-6729 holds simultaneously.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog reaches and resolves the branch (T=3 on the W side at L6728) and its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs whose bytes are progressively closer to the three target characters, letting the corpus accrete partial-match seeds (e.g. the 'A'-bearing DTD/keyword bytes visible in the winning seeds) until all three positions land on 'ANY'.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog never takes the true side (loser blocked=10/10, T=0 F=271 at L6728 on the L side): with only edge-bucket + I2S feedback, the three-character keyword has no single intercepted CMP whose operand it can splice, and partial 1-of-3 / 2-of-3 matches earn no new coverage, so the corpus never gets the gradient pressure that finishes the keyword.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile's CMP_MAP gradient (Hamming/prefix-distance buckets) is the sole technique vpc adds over cmplog; it gives partial-match credit toward the multi-byte 'ANY' literal that the edge-only + I2S signal in cmplog cannot reward, so the value-profile feedback is what carries the corpus across the gate.

evidence_trail.claim: Taking the blocked true side requires the three-byte literal keyword 'ANY' at the cursor.
The match is a chained three-character equality including the NXT(2)=='Y' clause.
value_profile_cmplog is the winner and cmplog the loser at this branch, differing only by the value_profile technique.
The winner resolves the branch in 8/10 trials while the loser blocks in all 10.
The loser never resolves: blocked in 10/10 trials.
On the winner side the true branch at L6728 is taken (T=3) whereas the loser side never takes it (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L6728  T=3 F=7  T=0 F=0  } else if ((RAW == 'A') && (NXT(1) == 'N') &&  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog's added feedback over cmplog is the CMP_MAP gradient buckets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's feedback is only the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no value-profile gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
**Feedback**: same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile), not I2SRandReplace, so db_query.py lineage dash-row verification is not the applicable signal; the technique delta is established directly from the DECISIVE PAIRS line 'value_profile_cmplog > cmplog [delta: value_profile]' and the MECHANISM CONTEXT showing vpc adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets that cmplog lacks. I did not run lineage because the I2S floor signal would not distinguish the value-profile contribution that separates these two I2S-equipped fuzzers.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on a single-byte literal 'A' (one CMP, no multi-byte chain) showing value_profile_cmplog and cmplog resolve at statistically equal rates would refute that the CMP_MAP gradient over a multi-byte keyword is what separates them here.

weakest_evidence_point: No seed lineage directly demonstrates a value-profile partial-match accretion path; the CMP_MAP-gradient attribution is inferred from the pair's technique delta plus source shape rather than from an observed gradient trail.
